# REGIME READ — 2026-06-23

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 2 → 3 (DEMOTED).** **Bearish trigger PARTIALLY FIRED: VIX daily close 19.62 (+13.54%) is at the upper edge of Stage-2 band (12-18) and has breached 18 decisively — combined with VXN re-spike from 26.31 → 27.67 (+5.17%), broad Nasdaq -1.32%, and 1-day vol-of-vol acceleration on a no-FOMC, no-data, no-news Monday — the "quiet hedging undercurrent" flagged Jun 22 has converted to overt repositioning. Stage 2 promotion was 5 trading days old; it has not survived first contact with PCE-week positioning.**
- **Total score: +2 → -1 (delta -3).** Three lenses flipped: Dealer-flow +1 → -1 (VIX/VXN re-spike), Breadth +1 → 0 (Nasdaq -1.32% / NDX -0.19% / SPX -0.37% — mega-cap divergence breaking; RUT +0.83% + DJI +0.29% can't offset Nasdaq weakness when Nasdaq carries 50%+ of risk-on tape), Cross-asset +1 → 0 (Brent extending lower is constructive but Gold continuing higher + DXY firming is now overt risk-off rotation, not a "quiet undercurrent").
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ daily close above $88.09:** **ARMED, untested** (no Jun 22 close retrievable; estimate carries forward).
  - **Bullish — VXN sustains below 22:** **CLEARED-NEGATIVE.** VXN moved AWAY from trigger (26.31 → 27.67). Spread to threshold widened from 4.3 pts to 5.7 pts.
  - **Bullish — VIX below 15 for 2+ sessions:** **CLEARED-NEGATIVE.** VIX moved AWAY (17.32 → 19.62). Spread to threshold widened from 2.3 pts to 4.6 pts.
  - **Bullish — F&G above 55:** **ARMED, unmeasured.**
  - **Bullish — Clean MOU signing Jun 19:** **STILL FIRED, but its tailwind has expired** (5 trading days post-event; geopolitical lens still +2 but no longer the dominant signal — Macro -2 + Dealer-flow flip -1 now overwhelm it).
  - **Bullish — DJI leadership rejoining:** **ARMED-POSITIVE Jun 23** (DJI +0.29% vs Nasdaq -1.32% — leadership flip is the wrong kind: DJI doing well because Nasdaq is selling off is defensive rotation, not risk-on broadening).
  - **Bullish — Benign PCE Jun 26:** **ARMED — 3 sessions out.**
  - **Bearish — TQQQ below 50DMA $76.39:** **ARMED** (no Jun 23 TQQQ close in snapshot; on est. ~$82 (3× NDX 30,347.08), cushion is ~+7.3% — narrower than Jun 22's ~+9.3%).
  - **Bearish — VIX sustained above 22:** **APPROACHING** (19.62, still 2.4 pts below trigger but +2.30 pts in one session = volatility-of-volatility regime change).
  - **Bearish — VXN re-spike above 30:** **APPROACHING** (27.67, 2.3 pts below trigger).
  - **Bearish — VIX daily close above 20:** **NEARLY FIRED** (19.62 closed 0.38 below).
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT FIRED** (no fresh print).
  - **Bearish — MOU walk-back / Trump tariff escalation:** **NOT FIRED.**
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer-flow +1 → -1 (-2 swing) | Breadth +1 → 0 | Cross-asset +1 → 0. All three flipped on the same session — coordinated repositioning, not noise.

> **Snapshot freshness note:** SPX/NDX/Comp/DJI/RUT/VXN = Jun 22 close. VIX/DXY/Brent/Gold = Jun 23 intraday. 10Y = Jun 18 stale (note: -5.0% vs 52W high signals yields are still elevated despite easing — this is a **near-52W-high yield environment, NOT a yield-easing environment**). DXY 101.28 (+0.25% Jun 23) is firming, having pushed back near its 52W high.
>
> **DXY reading correction:** DXY at 101.28 vs 52W high 101.29 means DXY is **at 52W high**, not -0.0% from a previously easing level. This is a meaningful dollar bid. Combined with 10Y still near 52W-high yields (4.451, only -5.0% from 52W high), the macro lens has hardened.
>
> **Brent reading:** Brent at 77.81 is -38.3% from 52W high $126.10 — Iran-relief release is essentially complete; no more downside fuel from this lens. From here, oil is range-bound at lows.
>
> **Gold reading:** Gold -1.03% Jun 23 reverses Jun 22's +1.24%, but Gold at $4,138.90 is **-25.9% from 52W high $5,586.20** — Gold has come off massively from peak fear. Gold's intraday wobble matters less than the bigger picture: it's still in major drawdown vs Q1 2026 fear peak.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 3 — Neutral/choppy (DEMOTED from Stage 2).** VIX 19.62 sits inside Stage-3 band (15-22) and outside Stage-2 band (12-18). VXN re-spike confirms.
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 23, 2026 — VIX/VXN re-firm + Nasdaq concentration breakdown. **Day 1 of Stage 3.**
- **Position:** Early Stage 3. **PCE Jun 26 is 3 trading days out** and now the binding macro decider. Path-dependence is high.
- **Confidence: Medium-High.** Demotion is supported by 3 lens flips on the same session + volatility-of-volatility regime change (VIX +13.5% on a no-news Monday is not noise). Three sessions to PCE means the demotion may either consolidate (Stage 3 holds, awaits PCE), or accelerate (VIX through 22, Stage 4).
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2 reclaim):** VIX close below 18 for 2+ sessions AND VXN close below 25 AND benign PCE Jun 26 (core PCE in-line/cooler + dovish market reaction).
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** VIX daily close above 22 OR VXN through 30 OR Nasdaq Composite close below -5% from 52W high (currently -3.8%, threshold ~$25,830) OR hot PCE Jun 26 OR HY OAS through 300 bp.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5 bypass):** Concurrent VIX above 25 + credit blowout + Nasdaq -7%+ from 52W high.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **demotes to Stage 3 neutral/choppy on coordinated repositioning Monday tape** — VIX/VXN re-firming, Nasdaq concentration breaking down, DXY pushing 52W high, Gold/Treasury bid continuing — the "quiet hedging undercurrent" flagged Jun 22 has converted to overt PCE-week defensive positioning, with PCE Jun 26 (3 sessions out) the binding decider for both direction and stage. Medium-high confidence.

VIX +2.30 pts in 1 session on no fresh news = vol-of-vol regime change. Term structure likely re-steepening. Score flipped -1 |

Hawkish FOMC dot 3.8% intact. The macro headwind that was "sticky" Jun 22 is now actively hardening |

## Diverging Signals
- **VIX +13.54% on a no-news, no-data Monday is the dominant signal.** Pre-PCE positioning typically shows VIX +0.3-0.8 pt in the 2 sessions before print (historical anchor). +2.30 pts in 1 session, 3 sessions ahead of PCE, is 3-7× the typical pre-event bid. This is either (a) **pricing in a hot-PCE scenario aggressively**, or (b) **PCE-positioning + something else** — credit, geopolitical chatter not in the snapshot, or institutional rebalancing into quarter-end (Jun 30).
- **Nasdaq -1.32% vs DJI +0.29% vs RUT +0.83% is overt defensive rotation.** Mega-cap concentration that powered the Jun 18 rally is unwinding. Stage 2 promotion was anchored on Nasdaq breadth; Nasdaq is now the leg breaking.
- **DXY at 52W high + 10Y near 52W-high yields + Gold still bid + Brent at lows = late-cycle defensive cluster.** This is not "risk-off-into-Treasuries"; it's "risk-off-into-dollars-and-rates-stay-high." The macro environment is hardening, not just sticky.
- **Geopolitical lens +2 vs everything else neutral-to-bearish is now the load-bearing offset.** If geopolitical reverts (any MOU complication, Trump tariff headline, fresh Iran chatter), the regime collapses below Stage-3 quickly.

IV (~83%) × 0.141 ≈ ±$9.59 ≈ ±11.7%. **Lower bound $72.4 sits below 50DMA $76.39 by $3.99 — a one-sigma down move now decisively breaks the 50DMA gate (Stage-3 demotion trigger).** Upper bound $91.6 sits above the 4-wk/52-wk high $88.09 by $3.51 — a one-sigma up move still probes new highs but requires a benign PCE catalyst. **Asymmetry shift:** the EM band now straddles the bullish/bearish gate, where Jun 22's band kept the bearish gate inside the band but with more cushion. EM expansion (+0.6 pts) + downside-asymmetry = elevated path-risk into PCE.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated ~60-65 — neutral, drifting down from ~65-70 on Jun 22 weakness. No fresh Jun 23 data.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS (held but cushion shrinking).** Estimated -6.9% from 4-wk high $88.09 (vs Jun 22's -5.2%); +20.4% above 4-wk low $68.08 (vs Jun 22's +22.6%). Still well above DOWNTURN threshold ($70.47) and below RALLY threshold ($88.50). No structural transition, but the SIDEWAYS box has tilted modestly toward the downside.
- **Trend:** Price estimated above both 50DMA ($76.39, cushion +7.3% — narrowed from +9.3%) and 200DMA ($68.09, cushion +20.4%). 50DMA is now the proximate gate; a -7% move closes it. EM lower bound $72.4 now sits below 50DMA — one-sigma adverse path breaks the gate.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN 27.67 (+5.17%) confirms re-spike** — Jun 22 NDX-vol release was incomplete and has reversed. Est. TQQQ IV ~83% (up from ~78.9% Jun 22). EM widened to ±11.7%. **VIX +13.54% Jun 23 is the volatility-of-volatility signal — premium is repricing more aggressively than realized vol justifies, classic pre-event behavior with a hedging bias.** Premium is rich (favourable for sellers in absolute terms) but is **rich for a reason** (priced-in PCE risk + defensive flow + Nasdaq concentration unwind).
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS (demoted from FAVOURABLE).** Demoted on: (a) **Regime demoted Stage 2 → 3**; (b) **VIX through 18, +13.5% in 1 session** — vol-of-vol signal, premium-bid is informational not just opportunity; (c) **VXN re-spike +5.17%, est. TQQQ IV ~83%** — premium is rich BUT priced into PCE risk; (d) **50DMA cushion narrowed to +7.3%** with one-sigma EM lower bound $72.4 now decisively below 50DMA $76.39 — adverse path triggers Stage-3-deepens and Stage-4-watch; (e) **Defensive cross-asset rotation** (DXY at 52W high, Gold continuing bid) confirms institutional positioning is defensive into PCE; (f) **PCE Jun 26 is 3 sessions out and binding** — hot print historically costs 1-2% SPX = ~3-6% TQQQ on 3× leverage + VIX spike of 1-2 pts (would push VIX through 22 = Stage 4 trigger); (g) **Geopolitical +2 is the only load-bearing offset** — single-point-of-failure if any MOU/tariff headline arrives. **Net: CAUTIOUS — the regime has demoted and PCE is binding inside the window. Macro lens still -2, hardening; F&G not reconfirmed. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.**

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2 reclaim):** TQQQ daily close above $85 (recovery of pre-Jun-22 levels) AND VIX close below 18 for 2+ sessions AND VXN close below 25 AND benign PCE Jun 26 (core PCE in-line/cooler + dovish market reaction).
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39 (one-sigma adverse path territory), OR VIX daily close above 22, OR VXN through 30, OR Nasdaq Composite close at -5% from 52W high (~$25,830, ~3% further down from current), OR hot PCE Jun 26 (core PCE > 3.0% YoY or hawkish reaction), OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR MOU walk-back / Trump tariff escalation.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5 bypass):** Concurrent VIX above 25 + credit blowout + Nasdaq -7%+ from 52W high.
- **Neutralizer:** A benign PCE Jun 26 + VIX re-easing back through 18 + Nasdaq mega-cap reclaiming leadership would re-promote toward Stage 2. The Stage 2 promotion (Jun 18) was fragile; reclaim now needs explicit catalyst-driven confirmation, not just absence-of-bad-news.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 23-25, 2026 (Tue-Thu):** Fed-speak window open; Micron (MU) earnings (date confirm — week of Jun 22-26 typical).
- **Jun 26, 2026 (Fri):** **PCE Inflation (May reading)** — **3 trading days from today; binding macro decider for both direction and stage.**
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** **Quarter-end rebalance** — outsized close-auction activity; ~5 trading days out. Pre-quarter-end positioning drift typically Jun 25-30.
- **Jul 3, 2026 (Fri):** NFP June payrolls — pre-July-4 close, ~8 trading days out.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 23 today, Jun 24, Jun 25, Jun 26, Jun 29):** Fed-speak likely; MU earnings; **PCE Jun 26**; quarter-end positioning begins Jun 25-26.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — PCE Jun 26 binding; quarter-end rebalance Jun 30 enters the 5-session window from Jun 25 forward (i.e., 2 sessions out by Jun 26).

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Last was **Jun 19, 2026** (passed, 2 trading days ago). Next is **Sep 18, 2026** (~62 trading days out). Outside relevance window.
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~5 trading days from today.** Inside the 5-session relevance window from Jun 25 forward. Expect outsized close-auction activity Jun 30 + month-end rebalancing drift Jun 25-30 (typically defensive bid into bonds + position trimming on winners).
- **Net:** **Quarter-end Jun 30 enters the 2-session relevance window starting Jun 26.** Combined with PCE Jun 26, the Jun 26-30 path is structurally event-loaded. Path Jun 23-25 is structurally normal but PCE-anticipation-loaded. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook
- **PCE Inflation (May reading) — Jun 26, 2026 (Fri, 3 sessions out).** Historical anchors for PCE prints in a comparable regime (sticky-inflation overlay + hawkish-Fed-dots reset + Stage-3 regime + elevated vol-of-vol pre-event): **Aug 31, 2023 PCE** (core 4.2% YoY in-line during sticky-inflation regime + VIX ~17 + VXN ~22 — SPX -0.2% in muted reaction, VIX -0.5 pt, brief relief; key learning: in-line during sticky inflation gives muted relief, not durable rally); **Sep 29, 2023 PCE** (core 3.9% YoY cooler during sticky regime — SPX -0.3% in pre-event drift, +0.6% on print, VIX -1.1 pt; the cooler-than-expected print gave constructive 2-3 session follow-through); **Dec 22, 2023 PCE** (core 3.2% YoY in-line during disinflation-celebration regime + VIX ~13 — SPX +0.2%, VIX +0.2 pt, holiday-thin tape muted reaction); **Apr 26, 2024 PCE** (core 2.8% YoY hot vs 2.7% expected during sticky regime — SPX -0.7% morning, recovered to +1.0% by close after dealers re-positioned, VIX +0.3 pt then crush 1.2 pt by Friday close; key learning: even slight hot prints get bought back when broader regime is risk-on). **Comparable-regime hot-print downside reference:** **Jan 26, 2024 PCE** (core 2.9% YoY hot during sticky regime + VIX ~13 — SPX +1.1% somehow, VIX -0.8 pt; this was an outlier — hot during a strong risk-on regime got absorbed). **More relevant Stage-3-style hot-print history:** **Apr 26, 2024 PCE** (core 2.8% hot vs 2.7%, SPX morning -0.7% then +1.0% recovery) and **Aug 30, 2022 PCE** (core 4.6% hot during early-2022 inflation scare + VIX ~25 — SPX -1.8%, VIX +1.6 pt; this is the closest stage-3-with-elevated-vol comparable).
- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through PCE Jun 26 in current Stage-3 vol-elevated regime means accepting: (1) **Pre-PCE vol-bid is already in flight** (VIX +13.5% Jun 23 = pre-event positioning + hedging activity 3 sessions out — typical sequence is more bid Jun 24-25 if pattern holds, VIX could reach 21-22); (2) **Print-day asymmetry in Stage-3 sticky-regime context** — in-line/cool PCE typically gives modest 0.5-1.0 pt VIX-crush + SPX 0-+0.6% on the day, with brief 1-2 session follow-through (Aug 2023 anchor); hot PCE in Stage-3-vol-elevated regime historically costs 1.5-2% SPX = ~4-6% TQQQ on 3× leverage + VIX +1.5-2.0 pt (Aug 2022 anchor) — this would push VIX through 22 and trigger Stage-4; (3) **NDX historically moves ~1.3-1.4× SPX into PCE in vol-elevated regimes** — TQQQ on 3× leverage = ~4-5× SPX-PCE-day move; (4) **Macro lens -2 + dollar at 52W high + yields near 52W high** means even a benign PCE doesn't unwind the broader headwind, only postpones it — historical pattern in 2H 2023 was modest 2-3 session relief into next macro print (NFP Jul 3 this cycle); (5) **Hot PCE → Stage-4 demotion trigger fires automatically** (VIX above 22 likely on hot-print magnitudes in this regime); (6) **Quarter-end Jun 30 enters relevance window from Jun 26** — even a benign PCE morning could see late-week selling if institutional rebalancing arrives early. **Net historical path estimate for in-line/cool PCE in Stage-3 sticky-inflation regime:** 2-3 sessions of constructive drift, VIX easing 1.0-1.5 pts, regime re-promotes to Stage 2 only if confirmed by VXN sub-22 in the following week. **For hot PCE in Stage-3 vol-elevated regime:** 3-5 sessions of risk-off retest, 50DMA gate $76.39 becomes binding for TQQQ, VIX through 22 triggers Stage-4 entry. **Asymmetry: downside is faster and deeper than upside.** **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**
